intro.text: You wake up in a dark forest, surrounded by tall trees. Where are you? Who are you? Your head hurts when you try to remember

apple.text: You are sure about something. You are HUNGRY. For how much time have you been asleep? In front of you, hanging from a tree, you see an unfamiliar fruit.

consequence.text: You LOVE it! Cruncy and sweet at the same time.

sword_1.text: You look around, trying to figure out what is happening to you, when your eyes are attracted by a sword. You feel a cold shiver down you spine...

consequence.text: Violence will not help you. You hand tries to reach the sword handle when you stop yourself and keep looking around.
Yes. Now you remember. You are a FEARLESS KNIGHT.

no_sword.text: With looking around, you notice someting in the dark of the woods. Two eyes. Looking directly at you! You freeze.

no_sword_detour.text: You retreat slowly. Your right hand is drawn to the sword. QUICK! YOU ARE IN DANGER!

consequence.text: You take the sword. In an instant the staring eyes disappear. Was it real? Did the sword scare the creature away?

crossroad.text: You look around. The sun is up, but how long before it's dark? You don't know. You notice you are in front of a crossroad. One path is full of brambles, while the other seems safer...

consequence.text: You take the safer path. If brambles don't grow here, probably more peolpe are walking on it.
A strong knight makes strong choices. You don't fear plants. You take you sword and start clearing your way on the hard path.

forest_loop.text: You keep walking. Following the path. You have been walking for a lot.

forest_loop_2.text: Yes, this must be the right direction!

forest_loop_sign.text: A SIGN! But what does it say?

consequence.text: You squint your eyes and try to read. It's clear, you need to turn left!
You don't need to read a gibberish sign to orient yourself in a forest. You take your sword and destroy the sign.

destroyed_sign.text: Whait! Why is the sign... bleeding?

continue_walking.text: It's a weird place, but it's getting dark, we cannot lose time on living objects. You continue walking

old_man.text: You notice an old man resting on a staff. He turns his head and stares at you

old_man_2.text: 'I wasn't expecting to see you again...' he says

consequence.text: 'Have we met each other before?' 'It's been a long time, but I will never be able to forget you. But why are you here? You seem... lost.'
'Who are you? How do I get out of the woods?!' You scream. The old man keep staring

old_man_3.text: The old man smiles at you in an uncanny way

old_man_4.text: 'This forest is surrounded by a river. Not water. But boiling pitch. And the smoke... it can confuse the mind.' Says the old man

old_man_5.text: 'Oh brave knight, the source of this river is located at the center of this forest, guarderd by a scary monster. Destroy the source and free us from this prison!'

old_man_6.text: 'How to reach it? This forest is alive and it always change. You need to follow the mist. Find the source!'

consequence.text: The old man points at a path. You take it.

You point your sword at the old man and try to attack him. 'WHAT'S GOING THROUGH YOUR HEAD?! We will see each other again, Wielburg...' Said the old man, disappearing into the woods. You take a deep breath and continue your walk.

old_man_gentle.text: 'Lost...' The old man nods slowly, as if confirming something to himself. 'This forest does that. Come, let me explain.'

path_to_source_1.text: The mist thickens as you follow the trail. Ahead, a small creature is trapped under a fallen branch, struggling in silence.

consequence.text: You crouch and carefully lift the branch. The creature wriggles free and vanishes into the undergrowth without looking back.
You slice the branch clean in two. The creature scrambles away, terrified, disappearing into the dark.

path_to_source_2.text: A narrow bridge crosses a ravine filled with black smoke. Halfway across, a cloaked figure stands blocking your way. It does not speak.

consequence.text: 'I only seek the source. No harm will come from me.' The figure tilts its head, then steps aside without a word.
You charge with your sword raised. The figure dissolves into smoke before you reach it. A hollow laugh echoes up from the ravine below.

path_to_source_3.text: The path is blocked by a wall of twisted thorns taller than you. Deep inside the tangle, two pale eyes watch you without blinking.

consequence.text: You stand still and breathe slowly. After a long moment, the thorns begin to curl back, opening a narrow passage.
You hack through the thorns. They writhe and bleed dark sap as you force your way through. The pale eyes vanish.

guardian_approach.text: The mist parts. You step into a vast clearing where a single enormous tree stands at the center. Its bark is twisted into something resembling a face. Its roots grip the earth like fists. It is alive, and it is watching you.

guardian_1.text: 'I WILL NOT LET YOU PASS, DEMON!' The tree's voice shakes the ground beneath your feet. Its branches coil like arms, ready to strike.

consequence.text: You lower your sword and raise an open hand. 'I am not your enemy.'
You charge forward, sword raised. A thick branch crashes down and coils around your sword arm, pinning it.

guardian_after_heart.text: The tree goes still. 'You... resist it?' A long creak, like old wood settling. 'Then prove it.'

consequence.text: You plant your sword in the ground and step forward with both hands open. The branches slowly uncurl.
Something surges through your arm. The sword rises on its own and you strike before you can stop yourself.

guardian_after_sword.text: The branch tightens around your sword arm. You are pinned. The tree's face lowers toward yours. 'Drop it. Or I crush you.'

consequence.text: You open your fingers. The sword falls to the ground. For a moment, the pressure eases — and then everything goes black.
You wrench your arm free with inhuman force and drive the blade deep into the trunk.

guardian_sh_ending.text: Without the sword, you are nothing in this forest. The branch tightens. The last thing you hear before the darkness takes you is laughter — coming from the sword on the ground.

guardian_calmed.text: The great tree exhales — a long, groaning breath. Its branches lower one by one. 'Go. But be careful, knight. What you carry wants to be fed.' Its roots retreat into the earth, opening the way.

guardian_killed.text: The ancient tree lets out a long, hollow moan and goes still. Its bark crumbles. The clearing is silent. You notice your sword hand is trembling — but not from effort.

source_approach.text: Beyond the clearing, the mist gives way to a faint glow. You follow it through the silence until you see it: a pool of black liquid, perfectly still, pulsing with cold light from below. The source.

source_deer_1.text: A small creature steps out from behind the pool. It looks like a fawn, but its eyes are too large and its legs too long. It stares at you with an expression that is almost human.

source_deer_2.text: 'STOP. Please.' Its voice is soft and clear. 'You must not destroy the source. The source is a seal. Every demon in this forest is bound inside it. If you break it, they will all be free, including the one living inside your...'

source_interrupted.text: A staff crashes down. The creature collapses without a sound. The old man stands over it, his smile unchanged. 'Pay no attention to monsters, Wielburg. Finish your work.'

source_choice.text: You stand before the source. The old man watches. Your sword arm inches toward the pool on its own. Your other hand grips your chest.

consequence.text: You reach for your heart — but your sword arm is faster. You watch your own hand plunge the blade into the pool. You didn't choose this.
You drop the sword and throw yourself into the pool. The seal must hold. You will be the lock.
The demon needed no convincing from you. Your arm was already moving.
You drive your sword into the pool. The seal shatters.

ending_source_destroyed.text: The pool explodes in black light. The forest screams. The old man laughs — a sound no longer human. From the mist, shapes begin to emerge. The demons are free. You have freed them.

ending_sacrifice.text: The black liquid rises around you, cold and absolute. The forest goes quiet. The old man screams — once — and then silence. Somewhere distant, a bird sings. The woods are still. You are the seal now.
